channel.title: Ben Chuanlong Du's Blog - Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Syntax of Teradata SQL
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

Syntax

  1. Each Teradata SQL statement must be ended by a semicolon.

  2. The keyword inner is optional in an inner join.

  3. To estimate the time and space complexity …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

Syntax

Please refer to Syntax of Teradata …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Docker on Windows
item.description:
It is suggested that you use Docker in WSL rather than using Docker for Windows.

While Docker can be used on Windows (10 and later) now, there are all kinds of issues. It is suggested that you avoid running Docker on Windows at this time unless you absolutely have to …

It is suggested that you use Python script to rename files in batch.

Change names of .txt files to lowercase.

rename 'y/A-Z/a-z/' *.txt

Get rid …

item.title: Synchronize Files Using Unison
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

https://incumbent.org/post/unison-sync-and-launchd/

  1. Unison对版本要求很高，进行同步的两台主机需要相同版本的unison， 所以这里使用和windows一致的版本2.13.16，unison-2.13.16.tar.gz

Configuration

默认的配置文件夹位于~currentuser/.unison，即当前用户的home目录下， windows则位于C:Documents and Settingscurrentuser.unison，默认的配置文件名是default.prf …

item.title: Use Eclipse as Your IDE
item.description:
1. There are at least 3 ways to jump to errors quickly in Eclipse

    • Click these red icons on the right margin.
    • Click the Previous Annotation button or Next Annotation buttons on the tool bar or under the Navigation menu.
    • Use hot keys (Ctrl+. for Next Annotation and Ctrl+, for Previous …

item.title: An Input Bug in Ruby
item.description: If you run a ruby program though Bash and the ruby program read multiple inputs from the console, all inputs will be the same as the first argument. I'm not very sure how to solve this problem.
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Send Email in Ruby
item.description: A few days ago, I started writing my first Ruby program which read my Verizon family plan statements, calculate bills for each member and send emails to notify them. The part of reading PDF document and calculating bill is easy. I stuck at the sending email part. There are many …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Tips on the Ruby Programming Language
item.description:
Array

  1. The method push inserts an element to the back of the arry; the method insert inserts an element to any place of the array.

  2. The method pop removes the last element of the array; the method delete removes any specified element from the array.

  3. The method concat combines the …

item.title: Use q to Query Text File Using SQL Syntax
item.description:
It is suggested that you use Python instead of q to manipulate text files!

Installation on Ubuntu

wajig install python3-q-text-as-data

General Tips

  1. The semantics of q is identical to sqlite. q preserve the original order of rows if no sorting (order by) is applied. You force querying by the order …

Tips

  1. Pig uses -- (at the beginning of lines) to comment out lines. It also support C-style comment, i.e., using /* ... */

  2. Filter out records that you don't want before you do expensive …

item.title: Install R Kernel for JupyterLab
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

  1. Install JupyterLab.
wajig install python3 python3-pip python3-setuptools
sudo -H pip3 install jupyter jupyterlab
sudo jupyter serverextension enable --py jupyterlab --sys-prefix
  1. Install dev libraries required by IRKernel (or …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Read PDF in Ruby
item.description:
"pdf-reader" is a good Ruby libary for reading pdf document. You can install it via RubyGems using the following command.

sudo gem install pdf-reader

You have to load the library before you use it. To load it, use the command below. 

require 'pdf-reader'

For more information, see the project on …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.description:

If you want to use a package that is missing in Cygwin (especially on the x86_64 version as some packages has not been ported to it yet), You can install it from Cygwin Ports following the instruction below if available.

  1. Download the newest version of Cygwin installtion …

item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: rJava Failed to Load rJava.So
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

rJava failed to load rJava.so and libjvm.so

Reconfigure Java for R using the command below resolves the issue.

sudo R CMD javareconf
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Develop R Packages Using "roxygen2"
item.description:
You have to install the R package roxygen2 first.

install.packages('roxygen2')

Roxygenize the package for compiling.

library(roxygen2)
roxygenize(path_to_package)

The following are some roxygen2 tags that I use frequently for writing R packages.

  1. Some R example code can take a long time to run. To prevent the illustration …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Zero-length Vector Issue in R
item.description: The corner case of 0-length vectors is not well considered in R. It causes issues in several situations. First, 1:n is probably not what you want when n = 0. Second, df$col = 0 throws error when df is an empty (0 row) data frame. To avoid these issues, it …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Traps in R
item.description:
1. Zero-Length Vector Issue in R

  2. A really tricky problem about is global and local variables. You can use any global variable in a user-defined function, which is true at least in R with version 2.13 or older. For example if you have defined a global variable a, you can …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

  1. The rmarkdown package is preferred to the markdown package. There is also a small bug with the markdown package. markdown::markdownToHTML does not render markdown …

item.description:

item.title: Cygwin Come to Rescue Linux Fans on Windows
item.description:

  2. Some tools (e.g., terminator) are not included in the regular release of Cygwin, but can be installed using Cygwin Ports.

  3. Configuration files for many applications (e.g., bash and Vim) in Linux can …

Backup

  1. Regularly backup your whole thunderbird profile! When your thunderbird encounters problems, you can recover it easily using your backup profiles.

item.title: Install the Latest Version of R in Ubuntu
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives. 

sudo apt-add-repository -y "deb http://cran.rstudio.com/bin/linux/ubuntu xenial/"
sudo apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys E084DAB9
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Install R Packages Behind Proxy on Windows
item.description:
There are 3 ways to install R packages behind a proxy on Windows.

  1. Download packages and install them locally. This is generally speaking not recommended as it is a hassle to resolve dependencies. When the package you want to install has no (or very few) dependencies, you can use this …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Install Jupyter-Scala for JupyterLab
item.description:
Note: Apache Toree is a better alternative than Jupyter Scala. It is suggested that you use Apache Toree instead of Jupyter Scala.

You can install Jupyter Scala following the steps below.

  1. Make sure Java and Scala are installed.

    wajig install openjdk-8-jdk scala

  2. Download coursier and move it to a searchable …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Install the rJava Package in R
item.description:
The most convenient way is to install the corresponding Linux package if exists.

sudo apt-get install r-cran-rjava

However, this Linux package is often outdated. Follow the steps below if you'd rather install rJava manually.

  1. Install OpenJDK and configure Java. 

    sudo apt-get install openjdk-7-*
sudo R …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Functions and Calls in R
item.description:
1. "[" is a function in R. It takes elements from a container (vector, matrix, list, etc.) For example, suppose lv is a list of vectors. The following code takes the first element of each vector in lv.

    lapply(l, "[", 1)

    In this way, you do not have to define a function …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Call Java in R Using Package rJava
item.description:
The package "rJava" offers a convenient way to call Java code from R. The following are some tips for using the "rJava" package.

  1. You must first start a Java Virtual Machine using .jinit before calling Java code from R. You can specifiy a vector of paths for the function .jinit …

install.packages("SVGAnnotation", repos = "http://www.omegahat.org/R")

item.title: Install Essential and Useful R Packages
item.description:

pkgs = ("data.table", "ggplot2", "RJDBC", "xlsx", "rmarkdown", "Rcpp", "inline")
install.packages(pkgs)
## biostatistics
source("http://bioconductor.org/biocLite.R")

update.packages(ask = FALSE, checkBuilt = TRUE)

unfortunately sometimes …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Writing R Extensions
item.description:
The following are some tips for using the old fashioned way (using .C and .Call) to write R extensions. The state-of-art way to extend R via C/C++ is to use the Rcpp package. Check this post For tips on using Rcpp package.

  1. If you want to call C code …

tidy_source('unformatted.r', file = 'formatted.r')

However, there is a bug in …

item.description:

  1. MULTISET Table

create table test ( id int, name char(20) )

当你用show table test检查表定义的时候发现，它已经变成如下定义： CREATE MULTISET TABLE test ,NO FALLBACK , NO BEFORE JOURNAL, NO AFTER JOURNAL, CHECKSUM …

item.title: Creating Index on Data in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

I am not sure whether creating index will reorder data or not ... it seems to me that it does modified the original data, but I'm not what …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Useful Add-ons for Thunderbird

  • Contact Tabs
  • Display Contact Photo
  • Google Contacts (for syncing contacts with Gmail)
  • gContactSync

Calendar

  • Lightning (comes with Task managing)
  • Provider for Google Calendar (use with …

SAS Stored Process

  1. %stpbegin and %stpend initialize the output delivery system (ODS). They are not needed if you don't have …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Advantages and Disadvantages of R
item.description:
Things on this page are fragmentary and immature notes/thoughts of the author. It is not meant to readers but rather for convenient reference of the author and future improvement.

Advantages

  1. R is opensource.

  2. Lots of new packages are being developed.

  3. Easy to learn, convenient to use.

Disadvantages

  1. R is …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: How MATLAB Is Different from Other Programming Languages
item.description: 1. MATLAB support command style (like Linux terminal commands). This means that you do not have to use parentheses when you call some functions, instead, you can use spaces to separate a function and its arguments. And when an argument of a function is string, you do not have to use …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Comparison of Values Involving NA in R
item.description:
The best way to compare values containing NA in R is to define customized comparison functions. Here is an example.

equals = function(x1, x2, na_as_na = FALSE, reverse = FALSE) {
    if (reverse) {
        return(!equals(x1, x2, na_as_na, reverse = FALSE))
    }
    if (na_as_na) {
        return(x1 == x2)
    }
    ifelse(
           is.na(x1), 
           ifelse(is.na(x2 …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

  2. Open a Cygwin terminal and run (the idea of this step is to use Cygwin Ports)

cygstart -- path_to_cygwin_setup.exe -K http …

item.title: Input and Output in R
item.description:
read.table, read.csv, read.delim, etc.

  1. It is suggested that you use read.csv/read.delim and avoid using read.table. This is because that the comment.char is off for read.csv/read.delim and on for read.table by default. My experience is that data seldom has …

  2. Function file.path …

  2. space(12) 12 spaces

Substring Functions

  2. right
  3. mid

or

item.title: Install the R Package "arrayQualityMetrics"
item.description:
I had to use the R package "arrayQualityMetrics" to check the quality of some gene chips. Installing this package in Linux takes a few steps. The following is the brief instruction on how to install R package "arayQualityMetrics" in Debian. 

# install the graphics library cairo
sudo apt-get install libcairo2 …

data.table do not mix using of data.table and data.frame (too much).

plyr

sets

set operations,

item.title: Format Numbers in R
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

When working with dates, it is desirable to format months and days as 2 digits numbers with a leading 0.

sprintf("%02d", 1)
# or 
formatC(1, width …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Find Elements That Appear Multiple Times in R
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives. 

freq = tapply(x, x, FUN=length, simplify=TRUE)
freq[freq > 1]
# or you can use
freq = table(x, useNA = "always")
freq[freq > 1]
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Missing Values in SAS
item.description: 1. SAS uses a dot (.) to stand for a numeric missing value and any number (can be 0 which correspond to the blank string "") of white spaces (e.g., " ") for character missing value. (This also means that you cannot save pure white spaces in SAS.) However, when you enter values after …

item.description:

  2. A missing character in SAS is represented by any number of white spaces. To see more detailed discussion on …

item.title: Limiting/Filtering Results in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

In the data step you can use the hidden variable _n_ to select records based on observation id.

In proc sql, you can use the outobs option …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Copy Data in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives. 

/* copy data using the datasets procedure */
proc datasets;
    copy in=src_lib out=des_lib index=yes;
    select data_set_1 data_set_2;
quit;

/* copy data using the copy procedure */
proc copy …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Transpose Data in SAS
item.description:
You can use the transpose procedure to tranpose a fat/thin data to a thin/fat data in SAS. For example, suppose we have a data set s1 in SAS.

data s1; 
    input famid faminc96 faminc97 faminc98 spend96 spend97 spend98 ; 
    datalines; 
1 40000 40500 41000 38000 39000 40000 
2 45000 …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Write R Code that Runs Faster
item.description: R is a popular statistical software which is famous for enormous amout of packages. The syntax of R is very flexible with make it convenient at the cost of performance. R is indeed slow compared to many other scripting languages, but there are a few tricks which can make your …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Manipulate Data in SAS
item.description:
Data Manipulation

  1. Create a user library if you want your SAS data sets to persist. For example, the following code creates a library named lib which points to the directory ~/sas/lib, and creates a data set named s1 in this library in the data step.

    libname lib "~/sas/lib …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: General Tips for SAS
item.description:
Things on this page are fragmentary and immature notes/thoughts of the author. It is not meant to readers but rather for convenient reference of the author and future improvement.

  1. Do not go crazy in encapsulating/reusing code in SAS. SAS is too stupid on this.

  2. proc univariate only works …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Add Sequential ID for Observations in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

data s1; set s; obs_id = n; run;

proc sql; create table s1 as select monotonic() as obs_id, * from s ; run;

  1. The monotonic function runs before sort and …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

  1. You can open multiple SAS Enterprise Guide instances. This allows you to work on another project if your SAS code takes a long time to run.

  2. You …

item.title: Control SAS Outputs
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

  1. it is suggested that you use ods select none; at the beginning of a user-defined macro to supress unnecessary output. and ods select all; at the end …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Compile MATLAB Code
item.description: 1. MATLAB code can be compile to C/C++ code or stand alone applications. Usually you can run the stand-alone application in Windows system directly, but for Linux system it is a little headachy. The stand-alone application usually takes less than 50% of the time that the original MATLAB code takes …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Import/Export Data to/from SAS
item.description:
Import Data to SAS

  1. The IMPORT procedure is based on the DATA step. When you run an IMPORT procedure, the unlying data step code is printed into the log. Sometimes the IMPORT procedure does a little bit extra work to automatically decide the most possible format you want (especially when …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Tuple in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

A tuple can be used both in a sql procedure or a data step. Values are usually separated by commas, e.g., (v1, v2, v3). However, commas …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Number of Observations in a SAS Dataset
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives. 

proc sql;
    select count(*) from lib.dataset;
run;

Proc sql is not efficient when we have large dataset. Though using ATTRN is good method but this can …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Create a Directory Using SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

you can use the function dcreate to create a directory in sas sas: created don't use "~" in the path, can cause problems

x mkdir path;

new-directory=DCREATE …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Formatting Data in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

Data Formatting

  1. To add labels to a variable, You can use the statement label to add labels to a variable in SAS. And it doesn't matter whether …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Date Utilities in SAS
item.description:
Convert a Date to a String of the Format "YYYYMMDD"

data _null_;
    x = put("23Aug2014"d, yymmddn8.);
    put x date9.;
run;

or

data _null_;
    x = putn("23Aug2014"d, "yymmddn8.");
    put x date9.;
run;

Both of the 2 data steps print "20140823". The following is a comprehensive list of expressions (that …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: SAS Loop
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives. 

y = 0;
do i = 1 to 10 by 2;
   y = y + i;
end;

count = 1;
do while(count<5);
   count = count+1;
end;

count = 1;
do until …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.description:

item.title: Rounding Related Functions in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

  1. To avoid numerical issues, you should use the ceil function instead of the ceilz function for rounding up generally speaking.

  2. Notice that the round function in SAS …

You can do this in two steps: 1) Use GLMSELECT as if you had an OLS model, and get several sensible models 2) Try those models in …

item.title: Run Unix/Linux Commands in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

SAS can run Unix/Linux commands everywhere using

x command;

For exampel, to create a directory named "abc" in your home directory.

x mkdir ~/abc;

It can …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: SAS Coding Style
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

  1. CamelCase for procedure names
  2. camelCase for other keywords
  3. end procedures with run or quit
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: R for Internet/Web Applications
item.description:
Sending/Receiving Emails

  1. There are many ways to send email in R. First, you can use sendmail{sendmailR}. The following code is a simple test email.

    sendmail(from="firedragon.du@gmail.com",
to="dclong@iastate.edu",
subject="Sending Email from R",msg="It works",
control=list(smtpServer="mailhub.iastate.edu …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Keep Matrix Structure when Extract a Row/Column from a Matrix
item.description: I talked in the post Abuse of Vector in R that it is often the case that we want keep the matrix sturcture when extracting a row/column from a matrix, i.e., we often want to return a matrix with 1 row/column when extracting a row/column from …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Easy Made Mistakes when Writing R code
item.description:
Being flexible is a two-side sword to R. While it make it convenient and productive to use R, it is also very easy to make mistakes when writing R code. The following is list of mistakes that happens all the time.

  1. Miss passed arguments
    This is often due the to …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Filesystem in MATLAB
item.description: 1. Function path can be used to either display or modify the search path of MATLAB. Its functionality in MATLAB is similar to the functionality of environment variable PATH for DOS shell in Windows systems, and the use of them is also similar. addpath can be used to add new folders …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Skills for R
item.description:
1. Unlike C/C++, Java and SAS, you do not have semicolon to indicate the end of a command, but you can do this if you like. It is helpful if you have to write multiple commands on a line.

  2. R support command/terminal mode, and if you open R in …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Generating Random Numbers in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

CALL RANDGEN( result, distname<, parm1><, parm2><, parm3>); or u = rand("Uniform"); / u ~ U[0,1] / based on Mersenne-Twister in a data step

forget about other random number …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Choose a Procedure for Modeling in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

  1. proc glm has many adavantages over proc reg and proc anova. For example, proc glm supports categorical variables while proc reg does not. It is suggested that …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: IBM AIX Questions
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

  1. lsp works well only in current directory, if you specify another directory it doesn't work very well ...
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: IBM AIX Tips
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

Display the total disk usage of the current directory.

du -lsm

Display the disk usage of sas data set files.

du -lsm *.sas7bdat

Calculate the md5sum of …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Calculating Quantiles in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

Use the quantile function. Don't use distribution specific function models.

BETAINV returns a quantile from the beta distribution CINV returns a quantile from the chi-squared distribution FINV …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: User-defined Modules in IML
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

  1. There are in general 2 types of modules. Modules with or without arguments. A module without arguments CANNOT return values. A module with arguments can return values …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Work With SAS in Command Line
item.description:
Things on this page are fragmentary and immature notes/thoughts of the author. It is not meant to readers but rather for convenient reference of the author and future improvement.

sas script.sas -l script.log
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Insert Rows Missing as Missing Records in SAS
item.description: I recent come across a problem. I have a table A in SAS with columns x, y and z. The table almost exhaust the Cartesian product of x and y but has some rows missing. I need to create macro variables with the Cartesian product of x and y as …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du
item.guid: tag:www.legendu.net,2014-10-13:/outdated/blog/set missing rows as missing data in sas/

item.title: Conversation Mode in Thunderbird
item.description: There is a Thunderbird add-on Conversation which enables you read emails in the Gmail-like conversation mode. However, it has some limitations. For example, many shortcuts do not work if you use the Conversation add-on. Actually, thunderbird has a built-in conversation mode. To enable the built-in conversation mode, you need to …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Good Thunderbird Settings
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

Settings

  1. Signature (use html)

  2. Sync Lightning with Google Calendar

  3. cache

  4. threaded view, view -> sort -> by time / threaded

  5. disable global search and index

  6. set reply to address

  7. message …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Useful Shortcuts for Thunderbird
item.description:
Message

  1. F8: Message Preview Pane
  2. CTRL + N: New email
  3. CTRL + O: Open email
  4. CTRL + SHIFT + O: Open in conversation
  5. CTRL + U: Email source
  6. CTRL + R: Reply to an email
  7. CTRL + SHIFT + R: Reply to all
  8. CTRL + L: Foward email
  9. CTRL + E: Edit email as new
  10. CTRL + ENTER: Send current email …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: How to Submit R Packages to CRAN
item.description:
Submit using web form or using ftp following the steps below.

  1. Upload the .tar.gz file using anonymous as log-in name and your e-mail address as password to ftp://CRAN.R-project.org/incoming/. "ftp" is prefereed to "sftp".

  2. Send a message to CRAN@R-project.org about it. The email …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: MATLAB for Visualization
item.description: 1. To display a new graph on on top of an old one, you can use command hold on. In this way, you can create multiple plots in a same window. Though plot can already do multiple plot, command hold on can be very useful when you want to add some …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Popular Functions in IML
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

dif: lagged difference, keep missing values, may or may not what you want, can use negative lag, always return a matrix, with columns corresponding to differences of …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Passing Arrays Between R and Other Programming Languages
item.description: A matrix or an array in R is essentially a vector with dimension attribute. For this reason, no matter you pass a vector, matrix or an array to an external call, you essentially pass a 1 dimensional array to the call. As a coinsequence, it's usually not conveneint to pass …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: RStudio Does Not Start
item.description: Rstudio is the best cross-platform IDE for R so far. It is a lot like the IDE of MATLAB. Once I came across the problem that Rstudio did not start. I search website for a solution but has no luck. I have seen broken configuration files prevent applications from working …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Tricky Problems in MATLAB
item.description: 1. Sometimes you might find that you can use some built-in function in MATLAB at first, but later you cannot use it any. If so, you should probably check whether you are doing parallel computing in MATLAB. Parallel computing in MATLAB can cause some problem. What you can do is to …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: String in MATLAB
item.description:
1. To display special characters (e.g. \n, \t and so on), you have to use sprintf to format it first. fprintf does the job of formatting and printing together.

  2. Unlike R, numbers in MATLAB will not be silently converted to strings when needed. You must use the function num2str to …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Tips for the IML Procedure in SAS
item.description:
1. End an IML procedure with quit;. You should never use run; to end an IML procedure because it is used for calling user-defined functions in IML procedures.

  2. Until SAS 9.3, the IML procedure does not support overloading functions or recursive functions.

  3. You can define functions in the IML procedure …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Download Debian Using Jigdo
item.description:
You can save a lot of time using Jigdo to download Debian image especially when you have an old version of the image.

  1. Mount the the old version of the image to a folder that you have access to, e.g.,

    sudo mount debian-testing.iso /mnt

  2. Go to Weekly Builds …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Time and Date in R
item.description: 1. The function as.Date and strptime convert string to dates. For these functions to work right, you must use the right format.
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Compare R with Other Languages on Data Manipulation
item.description:
Here I compare R with other languages such as C/C++, Java, MATLAB, Python and so on data manipulation. I list a few advantages and disadvantages of R on data manipulation compared with other programming languages.

Generally speaking, it is very convenient to manipulate data in R. Using function read …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Program Termination in SAS
item.description:
Things under legendu.net/outdated are outdated technologies that the author does not plan to update any more. Please look for better alternatives.

To print an error message into the SAS log

%put ERROR: error_message;

To print a warning message into the SAS log

%put WARNING: warning_message;

To print a …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Cartesian Product in SAS
item.description: Suppose we have 2 variables x = ("a", "b", "c") and y = (1, 2, 3), and we want to create a dataset containing the Cartesian product of x and y in SAS. This can be achieved by manually input the Cartesian product of x and y in a data step. However …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Check the Type of a SAS Variable
item.description:
Things on this page are fragmentary and immature notes/thoughts of the author. It is not meant to readers but rather for convenient reference of the author and future improvement.

  1. SAS has only 2 data types, numerical and character. Date, time and datetime, etc. are all represented as numerical values …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: General Tips for MATLAB
item.description:
Skills

  1. MATLAB support profiling running time of code, so that you what to work on to optimize your code.s

  2. It is expensive to allocate a new block of memory (not only in MATLAB but also in other programming laguages), so you should avoid allocating new blocks of memory all …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Rcpp Rocks
item.description:
Please see here for some simple examples for learning Rcpp and related packages.

Tips

  1. Though you can overload functions in a Rcpp module, it causes problems when you export these overloaded functions. This is because currently Rcpp cannot distinguish them when exporting them. It is suggested that you do not …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Parallel Computing in MATLAB
item.description: 1. It is very easy to do parallel computing in MATLAB. Usually what you have to do is to change for to parfor for the loop that you want to run in parallel. The parallel code can also be run on a single thread and you will not lose much efficiency …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Feedly Tips
item.description:
Things on this page are fragmentary and immature notes/thoughts of the author. Please read with your own judgement!

  1. many sites doesn't offer feedly as an RSS option, but you can add feed links directly (similarly as in Thunderbird)
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Cheatsheet for Muttator
item.description:
Things on this page are fragmentary and immature notes/thoughts of the author. Please read with your own judgement!

Adopted from http://waxandwane.org/muttator.html

A Muttator Reference Sheet / Cheat Sheet

Muttator v0.6 for Thunderbird v3.0 and 3.1.* (muttator-20100629.xpi)

The main thing to know about …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Config Cymail in Thunderbird
item.description:
Things on this page are fragmentary and immature notes/thoughts of the author. Please read with your own judgement!

  1. Incoming Mail (IMAP) Server - Requires SSL: imap.gmail.com

  2. Port: 993

  3. Requires SSL:Yes

  4. Outgoing Mail (SMTP) Server - Requires TLS: smtp.gmail.com

  5. Port: 465 or 587

  6. Requires SSL: Yes

  7. Requires …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Some Tips for PHP
item.description:
Things on this page are fragmentary and immature notes/thoughts of the author. Please read with your own judgement!

  1. You'd better treat PHP as a case sensitive language.

  2. You can include a HTML template using the function include.

  3. scandir gets all files in a directory
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Some Links for ABook
item.description:
Things on this page are fragmentary and immature notes/thoughts of the author. Please read with your own judgement!

  • abook - Address Book - Linux TUI - YouTube
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Some Links for R

Things on this page are fragmentary and immature notes/thoughts of the author. It is not meant to readers but rather for convenient reference of the author and future improvement.

  • Experience with R
  • CRAN Task Views

item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Hide 3D Graph in Mathematica
item.description:
Things on this page are fragmentary and immature notes/thoughts of the author. Please read with your own judgement!

EdgeForm[] and Opacity[]
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: MATLAB IDE
item.description:
1. Command help offers help on functions, classes, packages and so on, but it support command style only, i.e. it can only be used without parentheses.

  2. The Shift+F1 hot key in command window opens the function browser in MATLAB. The F1 hot key opens the help document for function …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Linear Algebra in MATLAB
item.description: 1. Unlike most other programming languages, to get elements of an array in MATLAB, you have to parentheses to specify index. See more information in this post. If you want to get or set elements of a cell in MATLAB, you have to use curly braces. If you use parentheses to …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Numerical Analysis in MATLAB
item.description:
1. fmincon finds minimum of constrained nonlinear multivariate function. fminunc finds minimum of unconstrained nonlinear multivariate function. fgoalattain solves multiobjective goal attainment problems; fminimax solves minimax constraint problems.

  2. When you use one of these functions mentioned above to do optimizations, you might want to pass extra parameters to the objective function …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Error when Launching MATLAB in Linux
item.description: See here for more information.
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Sending Email in MATLAB
item.description:
It is very easy to send emails in MATLAB. To do this, you can just call sendmail. However, usually you need to set preference for sending emails using setpref first. Here is one example:

%%
% Define these variables appropriately:
mail = 'username@gmail.com'; %Your GMail email address
password = 'password'; %Your GMail …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Special Functions in MATLAB
item.description:
1. sin calculates sine of arguments in radian while sind calculates sine of arguments in degrees; asin calculates inverse of sine resulting in radian while asind calculates inverse of sine resulting in degrees. Other triangular functions behave similarly.

  2. The modified Bessel function of the first kind $I_n(x)$ is implemented as …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: MATLAB for Statisticians
item.description: 1. There are generally two ways to generate uniform and normal random numbers in MATLAB. One ways is to use [functions] randn (for standard normal distribution) and rand (for standard uniform distribution). The other way is to use [functions] unirnd and normrnd (there are also functions end with "rnd" for generating …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: R System and Configurations
item.description: 1. To allow opening R from a directory in R in Windows system, we can modify the registry. More specifically, we can add a command name (to be appear in the right-click menu) under HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\Directory\Backgroup\shell, and then add a subkey command under it. Last, we just need to …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Load User-written Script into Ruby
item.description: By default the current working directoly is not in the search path of Ruby. To load a user-written script named "script.rb" into Ruby, you can use the command require './script.rb'.
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Make Plots in SAS
item.description:
1. There are functions to add points, lines and legends to an existing plot in R. In SAS, there is no way to do this. What you can do is to add plotting commands in the plotting procedure and rerun your code.

  2. Staring from version 9.2, SAS offers new procedures …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du

item.title: Combination and Permutation Related Functions in R
item.description:
1. expand.grid creates a data frame from all possible combinations of supplied vectors or factors. For example,

    > expand.grid(letters[1:2], 1:3, c("+", "-"))
   Var1 Var2 Var3
1     a    1    +
2     b    1    +
3     a    2    +
4     b    2    +
5     a    3    +
6     b    3    +
7     a    1    -
8 …
item.dc:creator: Ben Chuanlong Du
